title: Psalms 133
subtitle: How Good and Pleasant When Brothers Dwell in Unity

timeline_link.text: See on Timeline — Song of Ascent

sections.header: Verses 1–2 — “Like Precious Oil on the Head”

heb.paragraph: "How good and pleasant it is when God’s people live together (yaḥad) in unity!" (v.1). Three verses of pure celebration. Unity is compared to two things: precious oil running down Aaron’s beard (v.2) — the anointing oil that consecrated the high priest, flowing abundantly; and the dew of Hermon falling on Zion (v.3) — heaven’s moisture descending on the holy mountain.

notes.note: "How good and pleasant it is when God’s people live together in unity!" The statement is simple; the rarity makes it precious. Unity among God’s people is both good (morally right) and pleasant (aesthetically beautiful).

"It is like precious oil poured on the head, running down on the beard, running down on Aaron’s beard, down to the collar of his robe." The oil flows from head to beard to collar — abundant, unstoppable, consecrating everything it touches. Unity is an anointing.

refs.note: Jesus prays that believers may be one, as he and the Father are one.
Make every effort to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
The early church was together, sharing everything in common—unity in action.

hist.context: Psalm 133 celebrates the blessing of unity among God's people. 'How good and pleasant it is when God's people live together in unity!' The pilgrimage setting is key: scattered tribes reuniting in Jerusalem for festivals. The psalm's brevity (3 verses) belies its importance for Jewish and Christian communities valuing fellowship.

sections.header: Verse 3 — “There the LORD Bestows His Blessing”

heb.paragraph: "For there (in unity) the LORD bestows his blessing (bərāk̅â), even life forevermore" (v.3). The dew of Hermon on Zion: refreshment from the highest mountain descending on the holy city. Where unity exists, God commands his blessing. The blessing is specified: life forevermore. Unity is the location of eternal blessing.

notes.note: "It is as if the dew of Hermon were falling on Mount Zion. For there the LORD bestows his blessing, even life forevermore." Where brothers dwell together, there — in that specific place — God commands blessing. Unity is the geography of blessing. MacArthur emphasizes the doctrinal significance of this passage within the framework of systematic theology. The text establishes principles that remain normative for the church — not merely descriptive of ancient Israel but prescriptive for how God's people should understand His character and respond in obedience.

refs.note: The holy anointing oil consecrates priests and tabernacle furniture for sacred service.
Believers have an anointing from the Holy One—spiritual unity through the Spirit.
With you is the fountain of life—the dew of Hermon brings life to arid land.

hist.context: The two images—anointing oil and Hermon's dew—both suggest abundance flowing downward. Oil on Aaron's head runs to his beard and robe; dew from Mount Hermon reaches even distant Zion. Unity among brothers is not just pleasant but sacramental (like priestly anointing) and life-giving (like dew on parched land). The final line locates blessing: 'there the LORD bestows his blessing, even life forevermore.'

rows.label: Commanded Blessing — 133:3
rows.text: "THERE the LORD bestows his blessing — LIFE FOREVERMORE." Unity is where blessing lives.

hebtext.note: "How good and pleasant it is when God’s people live together (yaḥad) in unity!" (v.1). Three verses of pure celebration. Unity is compared to two things: precious oil running down Aaron’s beard (v.2) — the anointing oil that consecrated the high priest, flowing abundantly; and the dew of Hermon falling on Zion (v.3) — heaven’s moisture descending on the holy mountain.

"For there (in unity) the LORD bestows his blessing (bərāk̅â), even life forevermore" (v.3). The dew of Hermon on Zion: refreshment from the highest mountain descending on the holy city. Where unity exists, God commands his blessing. The blessing is specified: life forevermore. Unity is the location of eternal blessing.

themes.note: Covenant peaks: unity as the location of commanded blessing. Three verses, two images, one theology: togetherness is where God acts.
